info.title: Ascribe Coder API
info.description:
To import this API to Postman, copy the URL from the top of this page.
Open Postman, and select Import.
In the dialog select 'Import From Link'.
Paste the URL and click Import.

To use this API interactively from this page, open the POST Sessions operation below.
Click the Example Value in the Parameters section to move it to the request.
Modify the request with your credentials, then click "Try it out!"
Copy the value of the bearerToken returned in the response to the api_key text box at the top of this page.
You can now interact with the other resources on this page.
You can save the bearer token for future use without needing to POST to the Sessions resource again.

post.summary: Initiates a new task to export AI Coder project data into an Excel file.
post.description:
This method exports AI Coder project data into an Excel file.

After the method triggers an export task, it returns a unique task key.

This key can be used for tracking the status of exporting through the API endpoint: **AICoder/Export/Excel/{taskStatusKey:long}**.

get.summary: Retrieves the current status of an Excel file export task.
get.description:
This method requires a task key as an input parameter, which is obtained from the response of the **AICoder/Export/Excel** method.
 
It returns the current status of the export task, including its result if completed.

If the task is not yet completed, the method should be called again after a delay.

Continue calling this method until the task is either completed or rejected due to an error.

parameters.name: taskStatusKey
parameters.description: The key associated with the ongoing task.

post.summary: Initiates a new task to export the generated codebook from an AI Coder project to a Coder study.
post.description:
It allows to export generated codebook in AI Coder back into Coder codebook by using some export strategy (Append, Overwrite, or Merge)

and determine whether responses should be coded automatically after the export.

After the method triggers generating codebook task, it returns a unique task key.

This key can be used for tracking the status of generating codebook through the API endpoint: **AICoder/Export/Codebook/{taskStatusKey:long}**.

Parameter **ExportType:** Specifies the strategy to be used for exporting the codebook.

Common options include:
  - **Append**: Adds the new codebook data into the existing codebook of Coder study.
  - **Overwrite**: Fully replaces the existing codebook in the Coder study with the new one.
  - **Merge**: Combines the new codebook with the existing one, replacing any existing codes with the same name and input/output IDs while adding new information

get.summary: Retrieves the current status of Codebook export task.
get.description:
This method requires a task key as an input parameter, which is obtained from the response of the **AICoder/Export/Codebook** method.

It returns the current status of the export task, including its result if completed.

If the task is not yet completed, the method should be called again after a delay.

Continue calling this method until the task is either completed or rejected due to an error.

parameters.name: taskStatusKey
parameters.description: The key associated with the ongoing task.

post.summary: Initiates a new task for analyzing study using Codebook Builder (UI-aligned v3 contract).
post.description:
This method analyzes responses from some study questions using Codebook Builder: prompt embeddings and clustering to build a prototype codebook.

Request `CodebookBuilder` options match the AI Coder app defaults and ranges (granularity 25–150, creation method AI vs data-driven,
minimum responses per code 1–250 default 3, classification strictness 75–95%, optional topic-based code cap 1–50 when enabled,
optional analyze-by closed-ended question).

Given codebook prototype can be used in applications such as Ascribe Coder with pre-editing it in AI Coder or without it.

Additionally, this process generates a structured dataset that can be utilized for:  
- Comprehensive analysis of the content of survey responses;
- Creation of detailed reports based on the analyzed data;  

This method enhances the efficiency of processing textual data and facilitates its interpretation.

post.summary: Initiates a new task for analyzing study.
post.description:
This method analyzes responses from some study questions by applying artificial intelligence (AI)

or special algorithms for automatically constructing a hierarchical model of a prototype codebook.
 
Given codebook prototype can be used in applications such as Ascribe Coder with pre-editing it in AI Coder or without it.

Additionally, this process generates a structured dataset that can be utilized for:  
- Comprehensive analysis of the content of survey responses;
- Creation of detailed reports based on the analyzed data;  

This method enhances the efficiency of processing textual data and facilitates its interpretation.

Note: For Codebook Builder (prompt embeddings) analysis aligned with the AI Coder app, use `POST /AICoder/Analyze/v3`.

post.summary: Initiates a new task for incremental analyzing study.
post.description:
This method does the same as **/AICoder/Analyze**, but for newly loaded responses only.
Optional `SuggestNewCodes` on the request body matches the AI Coder **Suggest new codes** checkbox when adding a load
(`addNewCodesWhenAddAICoderLoad`); default is `false`.

get.summary: Retrieves a status of analyzing (incremental analyzing) study task.
get.description:
This method expects a task key as an input parameter from the **/AICoder/Analyze** or **/AICoder/Analyze/Incremental**.

It returns information about current status of the analyzing task or result.

parameters.name: taskStatusKey
parameters.description: The key of the executing task.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of available codebooks from AI Coder projects.
get.description:
This method scans AI Coder projects for non-deleting codebooks and returns list of their defenitions.

If value of **id** property ends with "GAI" it means that codebook was created by using generative artificial intelligence.

If value of **id** property ends with "AI" it means that codebook was created by using simple artificial intelligence.

If value of **id** property does not include "GAI" or "AI" in the tail it means that codebook was created without using any kind of artificial intelligence.

post.summary: Initiates a new task for grouping codes into target net.
post.description:
This method re-builds the entire codebook of specified AI Coder project or its part.

Parameter **NetKey** allows specifying a child net as the root of a branch and rebuilding only that part.
 
If **NetKey** is set to **0**, the entire codebook will be processed.

Setting the **NetLevel** parameter to **0** removes all nets, otherwise this value must be positive and can be less than or greater than the current net level of the codebook.

get.summary: Retrieves a status of grouping codes to target net task.
get.description:
This method expects a task key as an input parameter from the **/AICoder/NetCodes** method.

It returns information about current status of the grouping nets task or result.

parameters.name: taskStatusKey
parameters.description: The key of the executing task.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of all AI Coder projects that available to current user.
get.description:
This method prepares a list of AI Coder project defenitions which available for the current user.

The result list includes deleted projects as well.

post.summary: Create new link(s) to an AI Coder project
post.description: This method generates temporary short link(s) for sharing. The link(s) leads to specified AI Coder project's page.

post.summary: Initiates a new task for exporting of dichotomous report
post.description:
After the method triggers exporting of dichotomous report task, it returns a unique task key.

This key can be used for tracking the status of generating of dichotomous report through the API endpoint: **AICoder/Export/Dichotomous/{taskStatusKey:long}**.

get.summary: Retrieves a status of exporting of dichotomous report task.
get.description:
This method expects a task key as an input parameter from the **/AICoder/Export/Dichotomous** method.

It returns information about current status of exporting of dichotomous report task or result.

If the task is not yet completed, the method should be called again after a delay.

Continue calling this method until the task is either completed or rejected due to an error.

parameters.name: taskStatusKey
parameters.description: The key of the executing task.

get.summary: Get a Codebook by its key
get.description:
Each Question has exactly one Codebook, which may be shared across multiple Questions.

A Codebook is tree-structured, where the leaf nodes are Codes and non-leaf nodes are Nets.  Only Codes may be used to code a Response.


This resource can return the Codes in the Codebook as a flattened list containing only the Codes, or as a tree with the full structure of the Codebook.

parameters.name: codebookKey
parameters.description: The Codebook key, which must be an integer value.

parameters.name: tree
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter.

            If true, return a tree-structured representation of the Codebook.
  
            When tree=false (the default) only the Codes are returned (no Nets) as a single list.

get.summary: Get the Codebook for a Question
get.description:
Each Question has exactly one Codebook, which may be shared across multiple Questions.

A Codebook is tree-structured, where the leaf nodes are Codes and non-leaf nodes are Nets.  Only Codes may be used to code a Response.


This resource can return the Codes in the Codebook as a flattened list containing only the Codes, or as a tree with the full structure of the Codebook.

parameters.name: questionKey
parameters.description: The Question key, which must be an integer value.

parameters.name: tree
parameters.description:
Optional query parameter.

            If true, return a tree-structured representation of the Codebook.
  
            When tree=false (the default) only the Codes are returned (no Nets) as a single list.

post.summary: Create a new Codebook for a Question
post.description:
This operation replaces any existing Codebook for the specified Question with the new Codebook.

This operation is permitted only if there are no coded responses in the Question.

parameters.name: questionKey
parameters.description: The key of the Question.

requestBody.description: The codes and nets in the new Codebook.

get.description: This operation returns the key and ID of all Companies in the Ascribe Account.

post.summary: Create a new Company
post.description:
The id assigned to the company must not already exist.

The maximum length of the id is 100 characters.
The id must not begin with an underscore character '_'.

requestBody.description: The information for the new Company.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of all scripts available to the current account, including global scripts.
get.description: Method returns a list of all script definitions which are available in current account, including all global ones.

scripts.name: Translation Complete Report
scripts.description: Uses the specified closed-end as language data to identify translation production by question

post.summary: Initiates a new export job
post.description:
Method initiates a new export job by using specified script and target questions and returns back a unique key of it.

The Job key should be used as inner parameter for **/Exports/{jobKey}** method.

get.summary: Retrieves a current status of specified job
get.description:
This method expects a job key as an input parameter from the **[POST] /Exports** method.

It returns information about current status of exporting of the job or result.

If the job is not yet completed, the method should be called again after a delay.

Continue calling this method until the job is either completed or rejected due to an error.

parameters.name: jobKey
parameters.description: Key of job

example.error: Message of excception if it occured while export operation
Message of excception if it occured while export operation
Message of excception if it occured while export operation

get.summary: Returns the languages supported for translation
get.description:
The Code for a language is used to specify the language when translating responses.

The Name for a language is suitable for display on a user interface.

get.summary: Get the Questions in a Study
get.description:
Retrieves by default all of the Questions in the Study.

You can optionally retrieve only a sub-set of the questions, filtering by the question type or the tasks assigned to the question.

parameters.name: studyKey
parameters.description: The key of the Study, which must be an integer value.

parameters.name: types
parameters.description:
Optional filter by Question type. If omitted, Questions of all types are returned. If present, a string containing these characters, the presence of which specifies the type of Question desired:
  • c: Closed
  • d: Data
  • o: Open
  • s: Other/Specify
  • v: Value
For example, the value 'so' (or 'os') returns Open and Other/Specify Questions.

parameters.name: tasks
parameters.description:
Optional filter by task. If omitted, Questions are not filtered by task. If present, a string containing these characters, the presence of which specified the task assigned to a Question:
  • c: Code this Question
  • x: Crosstab this Question
  • b: Transcribe this Question
  • t: Translate this Question
For example, the value ('ct' or 'tc') returns Questions with the Code or Translate tasks set.

post.summary: Creates new questions in the specified study by using one call to API
post.description:
This method expects an array of questions to create in specified study. question.type property uses following coding schema:
  • c: Closed
  • d: Data
  • o: Open
  • s: Other/Specify
  • v: Value
For example, the value 'so' (or 'os') returns Open and Other/Specify Questions. question.tasks property uses following coding schema:
  • c: Code this Question
  • x: Crosstab this Question
  • b: Transcribe this Question
  • t: Translate this Question
For example, the value ('ct' or 'tc') adjust Question with the Code or Translate tasks set. question.codingSource and question.alternativeCodingSource properties use following codes:
  • v: Verbatim
  • b: Transcription
  • t: Translation
  • empty string: None

parameters.name: studyKey
parameters.description: Key of target study

requestBody.description: Batch of question definitions

get.summary: Get the Responses in the specified Question
get.description:
For coded responses the keys of the applied codes are returned for each response.

These keys can be matched to the keys of the codes returned in a query for the codebook to find the details of each of the applied codes.


The optional date query parameters are UTC date/time values.

These should be specified in the form:

YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss

Where the hour ranges from 0 to 23.  For example:

2020-02-23T16:05:17

If the time portion of the date/time value is omitted it is assumed to be 00:00:00 GMT.

parameters.name: questionKey
parameters.description: The key of the Question, which must be an integer value.

parameters.name: includeText
parameters.description:
If true, the text of each Response is returned.

            If false or omitted the text is not returned.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
If omitted, all Responses for the Question are returned. If present, one of these values:
  • coded: return only Responses with at least one Code applied.
  • uncoded: return only Responses with no Code applied.

parameters.name: codedAfter
parameters.description: If present, only responses with a code applied on or after the specified date/time are returned.

parameters.name: codedBefore
parameters.description: If present, only responses with a code applied before the specified date/time are returned.

parameters.name: loadedAfter
parameters.description: If present, only responses loaded on or after the specified date/time are returned.

parameters.name: loadedBefore
parameters.description: If present, only responses loaded before the specified date/time are returned.

responses.text: I do not like the red color

post.summary: Add Responses to a Question
post.description:
This operation will add new responses to the specified question, optionally autocoding them. It will never modify existing responses.

For each QuestionResponse submitted, if the question does not have a response stored in Ascribe with the specified respondent id,

then the response will be added.
 
Otherwise, the Response stored in Ascribe will not be modified. The QuestionResponse submitted will be ignored,

and any codes that have been applied to the Response will be unchanged.


This operation allows the use of a Rule Set to modify or veto the Responses submitted, and automatically coding the responses as they are added.

parameters.name: questionKey
parameters.description: The key of the question, which must be an integer value

requestBody.description: The responses to add

responses.verbatim: I do not like the red color
I do not like the red color
I do not like the red color

put.summary: Add or update Responses in the specified Question
put.description:
If the Question does not have a Response stored in Ascribe with the specified respondent id, then the response will be added.
 
Otherwise, the Response stored in Ascribe will be updated if any of the properties of in the request differ from the values of the current properties of the Response.

parameters.name: questionKey
parameters.description: The key of the Question, which must be an integer value.

requestBody.description: The Responses to add or update.

responses.verbatim: I do not like the red color
I do not like the red color
I do not like the red color

post.summary: Create a new session for interaction with this API
post.description:
The credentialed user must have Adminstrator privilege in Ascribe.

The response message contains two tokens, one of which must be passed in a header for all other operations with this API.

You may either use the bearerToken or the authenticationToken.  Either send a header with key "authentication" and value the authenticationToken returned in this response,
or send a header with key "Authorization" and value the bearerToken returned in this response.

The bearerToken does not expire, but will become invalid if the user's credentials change.

The authenticationToken remains valid until no request is sent to this API using the authenticationToken for 30 minutes.

requestBody.description: The credentials for the session.

get.description:
The list of Studies returned may be optionally filtered by id, status, and client company.

When filtering by id the asterisk * character may optionally be used as a leading or trailing wildcard.

parameters.name: idFilter
parameters.description:
If omitted, the returned Studies are not filtered by id. Otherwise filter the returned Studies by this case-insensitive value. Leading and/or trailing asterisk characters (character code 42) may be used:
  • Leading and trailing asterisk : study id must contain the string between the asterisk characters.
  • Leading asterisk: study id must end with the string after the asterisk character.
  • Trailing asterisk: study id must start with the string before the asterisk character.
  • No leading or trailing asterisk: study id must match the specified value.
Any asterisk character which is not the first or last character of the value is considered to be part of the requested id.

parameters.name: statusMask
parameters.description:
If omitted, Studies with any status are returned. If present, a string containing these characters, the presence of which specifies the status of the Studies desired:
  • a: Archived
  • p: Coding in progress
  • c: Completed
  • d: Deleted
  • h: On hold
  • r: Review in progress
  • s: Shipping
  • u: Under construction
For example, the value 'pc' (or 'cp') returns Studies with 'Coding in progress' or 'Completed' status.

parameters.name: clientCompanyKey
parameters.description:
If omitted or null, the Studies returned are not filtered by Company.

Otherwise, the Studies returned are those assigned to the Company with the specified key.

studies.name: An example of study
studies.description: Use this study only for testing

post.summary: Create a new study
post.description: Method creates a new study based on data from request body.

requestBody.description: Information about the Study to create.

put.description: Method updates an existing study by using data from request body.

requestBody.description: Information about the Study to update.

delete.description: Method deletes the study by its key.

requestBody.description: Information about the Study to delete.

get.description: Method returns a definition of requested study by using its key.

parameters.name: studyKey
parameters.description: The key of the desired Study, which must be an integer value.

post.summary: Translate all Responses in a Study
post.description:
The method scans all questions in the specified study and checks for the **Translate Task** option.

Questions with this option enabled are included in the translation process to the target language.

For each of the question the method scans all untranslated yet responses and translates them.
 
At the end of the translation operation, the method provides statistics, including the number of responses translated

and the number of transactions performed for each translated question.

codes.description:
The Codes in the Codebook, in the order they appear in the Codebook.
  
Codebooks are tree structured and may be of any depth, although a depth of more than 6 is rare.
  
Only the leaf nodes may be applied to a response.  These leaf nodes are Codes.

Nets have child codes or nets.  Nets may not be used to code a response.  They serve to provide structure to the Codebook.

Nets typically represent groups of related codes.

AddCodesIntoCodebookRequest.description: A codebook presentation to create for specified question

codebook.description:
The Codes in the Codebook, in the order they appear in the Codebook.
  
Codebooks are tree structured and may be of any depth, although a depth of more than 6 is rare.
  
Only the leaf nodes may be applied to a response.  These leaf nodes are Codes.

Nets have child codes or nets.  Nets may not be used to code a response.  They serve to provide structure to the Codebook.

Nets typically represent groups of related codes.

codebooksAndTheirCodes.description: A list of codebooks and their codes to add

AddCodesIntoCodebooksBatchRequest.description: Request contains a list of codebooks and their codes to add

codebooksBatch.description: Array of result items of batch adding of codes into codebooks

AddCodesIntoCodebooksBatchResponse.description: Response contains information about added codes into a linked codebook or error message

codebook.description:
The Codes in the Codebook, in the order they appear in the Codebook.
  
            Codebooks are tree structured and may be of any depth, although a depth of more than 6 is rare.
  
            Only the leaf nodes may be applied to a response.  These leaf nodes are Codes.

            Nets have child codes or nets.  Nets may not be used to code a response.  They serve to provide structure to the Codebook.

            Nets typically represent groups of related codes.

errorMessage.description: Error message why codes can not be added into the Codebook

AddCodesIntoCodebooksBatchResultItem.description: Result item with information about status of adding codes into the specified codebook

codeKey.description: The key of the code, corresponding to the key of a code in the codebook.

description.description: The text description of the code.

AddedCode.description: A code automatically applied to the response.

AddedResponseBatchResult.description: Class contains information about newly created responses for target questions

rid.description:
The respondent id, which is unique among Responses in this Question.

This id identifies the respondent in the Study, and that respondent's Response in this Question.

verbatim.description:
The verbatim text of the response, in other words the answer the respondent gave to the question posed.

Required if this is a new respondent, optional if updating an existing response for this respondent.

translation.description: Optional translation of the verbatim text.

notes.description: Optional notes associated with the response.

codes.description: The codes automatically applied to the response.

codeKey.description: The numeric key of the code from the closed-ended question's codebook.

codeIsAbout.description: Optional. A hint to the AI about the meaning or topic of this specific code.

AnalyzeByClosedEndCodesOption.description: Represents a specific code to consider when using the CoderApiV2.Models.Coder.AnalyzeByClosedEndOption.

questionKey.description: Required. The numeric key of the closed-ended question to analyze responses by.

codes.description:
Optional. A list of specific codes from the question's codebook (CoderApiV2.Models.Coder.AnalyzeByClosedEndCodesOption) to focus the analysis on.
If omitted or empty, the AI may consider all codes or infer themes based on the overall responses for the question.

AnalyzeByClosedEndOption.description: Configuration for generating themes based on responses grouped by a closed-ended question.

autoCodeInputId.description:
If set to True, it will attempt to Auto Code based on a code's Input ID (Closed ends and Other Specifies only).

This AutoCode will execute after the Text Match and before the Regular Expression match, if either is enabled.

autoCodeByRegex.description:
If set to True, it will attempt to Auto Code responses via the codes' Regular Expressions.

This AutoCode will execute after the Text Match and Input ID match, if enabled.

autoCodeTextMatch.description:
If true, this will attempt to AutoCode responses based on an exact (but case insensitive) text match with previously-coded responses.
This AutoCode will execute first, followed by (if requested) Input ID matching and Regular Expression matching.

AutoCodeQuestionRequest.description: A request to autocode a question

AutoCodeQuestionResponse.description: Returns information about results of AutoCode operation

key.description:
The key of the Code.

This key is used to relate a coded response to its Code in the Codebook.

description.description:
The description of the Code.

This description represents the concept expressed in the coded Response.

inputId.description:
A optional identifier which may be used during automatic coding of responses.
  
If null this field will not be present in the response.

outputId.description:
A optional identifier which may be used to identify the code in tabulation software.
  
If null this field will not be present in the response.

regexPattern.description: Optional regular expression pattern which may be used for assisting human coders or for automatic coding.

color.description:
An optional color for the code in CSS hexadecimal six digit format, e.g. #FF0000 for red.

If the code color is the default value of #000000 (black) this field will not be present in the response.

children.description:
The children of a Net.

When querying for a codebook, this field will be present in the response only for Nets, and will never be present
when the Codebook is requested with the tree=false parameter.

The children are listed in the order they appear in the Codebook.

CodeOrNet.description: A Code or Net in a Codebook.

CodebookBuilderCreationMethod.description: How the Codebook Builder should derive themes and codes from open-ended responses.

codebookGranularity.description: Approximate target number of codes in the generated codebook.

minimumResponsesPerCode.description: Minimum number of responses that should support a code (segment frequency threshold for Codebook Builder).

classificationStrictness.description: How strictly open-ended responses must align with a code during classification, expressed as a percentage (higher is stricter).

maxTopicBasedCodesEnabled.description: When true, limits how many topic-based codes may appear in the codebook.

maxTopicBasedCodes.description: Maximum number of topic-based codes when CoderApiV2.Models.Coder.CodebookBuilderV3Options.MaxTopicBasedCodesEnabled is true.

CodebookGranularityOption.description: Defines the desired level of granularity (detail) for the automatically generated codebook themes.

minSegmentsPerCode.description: Minimum number of segments per code

The Codes in the Codebook, in the order they appear in the Codebook.
  
Codebooks are tree structured and may be of any depth, although a depth of more than 6 is rare.
  
Only the leaf nodes may be applied to a response.  These leaf nodes are Codes.

Nets have child codes or nets.  Nets may not be used to code a response.  They serve to provide structure to the Codebook.

Nets typically represent groups of related codes.

Identifying key for the Company.

This key will not change for the lifetime of the Company.

id.description:
The id of the Company.
  
This id is unique among Companies, but may be changed by the user.

The Codes in the Codebook, in the order they appear in the Codebook.
  
Codebooks are tree structured and may be of any depth, although a depth of more than 6 is rare.
  
Only the leaf nodes may be applied to a response.  These leaf nodes are Codes.

Nets have child codes or nets.  Nets may not be used to code a response.  They serve to provide structure to the Codebook.

The Codes in the Codebook, in the order they appear in the Codebook.
  
Codebooks are tree structured and may be of any depth, although a depth of more than 6 is rare.
  
Only the leaf nodes may be applied to a response.  These leaf nodes are Codes.

id.description:
The id for the new Company, which must be unique among all Companies by case insensitive comparison.

The id provided will be trimmed of leading and trailing whitespace.
  
The trimmed value must be from 1 to 100 characters in length and may not start with an underscore character '_'.

CreateNewCompanyRequest.description: Request to create a new Company

key.description: The key assigned to the new Company.

id.description: The id of the new Company, which is trimmed of leading and trailing whitespace.

CreateNewCompanyResponse.description: Response contains information about the newly created company

codebookKey.description:
An optional key of an existing Codebook.

Use this to specify that the new Question should share the Codebook of an existing Question.

For example, to specify that this new Question should share the Codebook of another Question in this Study, 
query the Questions resource to find the desired existing Question.  Assign the codebookKey property in this request the same
value as the codebookKey of the existing Question.

id.description:
The id of the new Question, which must be unique by case insensitive comparison among all Questions in the Study.

The value provided here will be trimmed of leading and trailing whitespace.  The resulting id must contain at least one character and not more than 200 characters.

label.description:
The label for the Question, which may be empty but must not exceed 25 characters.

The label is used as a short identifier for the Question in reports when the full id of the Question would be too long.

text.description: The text of the Question posed to the respondent, which may be empty.

help.description: The help information for the Question, which may be empty.

sortOrder.description:
Ordinal number of the Question.

When a list of Questions are displayed in Ascribe they are sorted first by this value, then by id.

CreateQuestionRequest.description: A request to create a new Question.

key.description: The key of the newly created question

codebookKey.description: The key of the Codebook for the Question. If you did not specify a codebook key in the request, this is the key of a newly created empty Codebook.

id.description: The ID of the new question

label.description: The label of the new question

text.description: The text of the new Question. This is the question posed to the respondent.

help.description: The help information for the Question, which may be empty.

sortOrder.description: Ordinal number of the Question. It uses for sorting questions in list, tables, so on.

CreateQuestionResponse.description: Response of Create Question web method, it contains data of the newly created question

CreateQuestionsBatchRequest.description: Request with inforamtion for creating multiple questions via one API call (batch operation)

CreateQuestionsBatchResponse.description: Response contains information about newly created study questions

codeKeys.description: Array of keys of codes in the specified codebook to delete

uncodeResponses.description: Delete codes from responses as well when it is true

DeleteCodebookCodesRequest.description: Request contains an array of code keys to delete from codebook

codeKeysDeleted.description: Array of code keys which were successfully deleted from the codebook.

codeKeysNotDeleted.description: Array of code keys which were not deleted by some reason. Usually such codes are applied to response(s).

codesRemovedFromResponses.description: Array of response-code pairs that were uncoded from responses in process of codes deletion.

DeleteCodebookCodesResponse.description:
Response contains two arrays with successfully-deleted and failed codes after the try of deletion.
Codebook code can not be deleted if it is applied for some response. Only unused codebook codes can be deleted.

DeleteQuestionResponse.description: Response of Delete Question API method

errorMessage.description: Error message why response can not be deleted

DeleteQuestionResponseResultItem.description: Result item with information about status of deletion of some response in the specified question

respondentIDs.description: Array of respondent ids to delete from the question

questionKeys.description: Array of pairs of question key with error message. Error message is null for successfully deleted questions.

entityType.description:
Type of entity (Codes/QualityCodes/Responses/Question). For example 'Codes' will remove all applied codes from all questions in questionKeys array.
To remove questions themselves, entityType 'Question' should be chosed.

deleteQuestionsBatchResults.description: Array of pairs of question key with error message. Error message is null for successfully deleted questions.

entityType.description: Type of entity (Codes/QualityCodes/Responses/Question). It duplicates value from request to the API method.

DeleteQuestionsBatchResponse.description: Response contains data about which question or question items were deleted in the batch operation.

errorMessage.description: Error message why response can not be deleted

DeleteQuestionsBatchResult.description: Question key and error message why question can not be deleted. Successfully deleted Questions has null for errorMessage property.

questionResponses.description: Array of pairs of question key and respondent id

questionResponses.description: List of result items of deletion of responses

respondentID.description: ID of the respondent that failed to be deleted

errorMessage.description: Error message describing why the deletion failed

DeleteStudyRespondentFailure.description: Represents a failure to delete a study respondent

respondentIds.description: List of respondent identifiers to be deleted

DeleteStudyRespondentsRequest.description: Request model for deleting study respondents

failedDeletions.description: List of Respondent Ids that failed to be deleted

notFoundRespondentIds.description: Array of respondents with no responses found

codebookBuilderPrompt.description: Optional prompt to guide the codebook building process.
codebookBuilderPrompt.example: Focus on identifying customer pain points and desired features.

applyRegEx.description: Determines of using regular expressions while data analysis

applyTrainingExamples.description: Determines of using training examples while data analysis

applyCodingRules.description: Determines of using coding rules while data analysis

ExistingCodebookOptions.description: Options for analyzing of existing codebook

text.description: The verbatim text of the response.  In other words, the answer the respondent gave to the question.

transcription.description: A transcription of the response, if present for the respondent.

translation.description: A translation of the response, if present for the respondent.

notes.description: Notes associated with the response, if present for the respondent.

codeKeys.description:
The keys of the codes applied to the response.

Use these keys to map codes applied to a response to information about each code using the information returned by querying the Codebooks resource for the question.

ExistingResponse.description: A respondent's response stored in the question.

createDate.description: Date when the job was created

startDate.description: Date when the job change status to Running

endDate.description: Date when the job change status to Complete

error.description: Message of error if it occured during the job's execution

ExportsJobStatusResponse.description: Class presents an information about current status of specified job

scriptKey.description: Name of script to use for new job

parameters.description: Additional job's parameters

exportAsText.description:
Request text format output instead of default Excel format.
Only works if the script has ScriptXlsxTextOption=true, otherwise parameter is ignored.
Cookie is always set: if not provided or false, exports as Excel; if true, exports as text.

ExportsRequest.description: Class contains necessary export options to create a job

ExportsResponse.description: Class contains an information of created job

errorMessage.description: Error message or reason why the code could not be updated.

FailedQuestionIdBatch.description: Record contains information about failed questins in batch operation and reason/error message why it failed.

FailedQuestionKeyBatch.description: Record contains information about failed questins in batch operation and reason/error message why it failed.

dataIsAbout.description: String that describing common sense of analyzing data

The Codes in the Codebook, in the order they appear in the Codebook.
  
Codebooks are tree structured and may be of any depth, although a depth of more than 6 is rare.
  
Only the leaf nodes may be applied to a response.  These leaf nodes are Codes.

Nets have child codes or nets.  Nets may not be used to code a response.  They serve to provide structure to the Codebook.

Nets typically represent groups of related codes.

GetCompaniesResponse.description: Response contains list of available companies

GetLanguagesToTranslateResponse.description: Response that contains list of languages that are supported by this API.

codebook.description:
The Codes in the Codebook, in the order they appear in the Codebook.
  
Codebooks are tree structured and may be of any depth, although a depth of more than 6 is rare.
  
Only the leaf nodes may be applied to a response.  These leaf nodes are Codes.

Nets have child codes or nets.  Nets may not be used to code a response.  They serve to provide structure to the Codebook.

responses.description: The requested responses in the Question.

GetResponsesResponse.description: The response of a query to the Responses resource.

name.description: A user-friendly display name of the Study.

status.description:
A one-character string which specifies the status of the Study:
  • a: Archived
  • p: Coding in progress
  • c: Completed
  • d: Deleted
  • h: On hold
  • r: Review in progress
  • s: Shipping
  • u: Under construction

supervisorKey.description: The key of the Person who will do revision of coding work

supervisorAssociateKey.description: The key of the supervisor's associate

clientCompanyKey.description: The Company to which this Study is assigned, or null if the Study is not assigned to a Company.

endCompanyKey.description: The key of the Company for which this coding work is being performed

help.description: Information to coders to assist in coding this Study.

createDateUtc.description: The UTC date/time the Study was created.

startDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was started.

dueDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was completed.

countQuestions.description: The number of Questions in the Study.

countResponses.description: The sum of the number of Responses in each Question in the Study.

countResponsesCoded.description: The sum of the number of Responses to which at least one Code has been assigned in each Question in the Study.

countTransactions.description: The number of transactions used by this Study.  Transactions are tokens of work performed on the Study.

GetStudyResponse.description: The response to a query for a specific Study.

GetUsersResponse.description: The users property contains a list of users.

minProximity.description: Minimum proximity value for merging codes

code.description: The language code, used to specify the language in API operations.

name.description: The name of the language suitable for display to the user.

NaturalLanguage.description: A natural language supported by this API.

description.description:
The description of the Code.

This description represents the concept expressed in the coded Response.

Maximum allowed length is 1000 characters.

inputId.description:
A optional identifier which may be used during automatic coding of responses.
  
Maximum allowed length is 50 characters.

outputId.description:
A optional identifier which may be used to identify the code in tabulation software.  
Maximum allowed length is 50 characters.

regexPattern.description: Optional regular expression pattern which may be used for assisting human coders or for automatic coding.

color.description:
An optional color for the code in CSS hexadecimal six digit format, e.g. #FF0000 for red.

If not specified the code color will default to black.

children.description:
The children of a Net.

If this list is not null or empty, this code will be a Net, and its children will be the codes or nets in the list.

The children will be inserted in the codebook in the order specified in this list.

NewCodeOrNet.description: A Code or Net in a Codebook.

id.description:
The id of the new Study.

Must be unique among all Studies in the Ascribe account of the current session by case-insensitive comparison.

Must be at least 1 character and not more than 30 characters.

name.description:
Optional name of this Study, intended to be more human-friendly than the id of the Study.

Maximum of 50 characters.

description.description: An optional description of the Study.

status.description:
Optional status of the Study at the time it is created. If specified, one of:
  • u: Under construction
  • p: Coding in progress
  • h: On hold
If omitted the default status is 'Coding in progress'.

supervisorKey.description:
The key of the Person who will do revision of coding work

If no supervisor is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

clientCompanyKey.description: Optional key of the Company for which this coding work is being performed.

endCompanyKey.description:
The key of the Company for which this coding work is being performed

If no end customer company is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

help.description:
Optional text to assist workers coding this Study

This may be used to provide customer specific instructions to the coders for work on this Study.

startDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was started.

dueDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was completed.

NewStudyRequest.description: A request to create a new Study.

name.description: A user-friendly display name of the Study.

status.description:
A one-character string which specifies the status of the Study:
  • a: Archived
  • p: Coding in progress
  • c: Completed
  • d: Deleted
  • h: On hold
  • r: Review in progress
  • s: Shipping
  • u: Under construction

help.description: Information to coders to assist in coding this Study.

createDateUtc.description: The UTC date/time the Study was created.

startDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was started.

dueDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was completed.

countQuestions.description: The number of Questions in the Study.

countResponses.description: The sum of the number of Responses in each Question in the Study.

countResponsesCoded.description: The sum of the number of Responses to which at least one Code has been assigned in each Question in the Study.

countTransactions.description: The number of transactions used by this Study.  Transactions are tokens of work performed on the Study.

NewStudyResponse.description: The response with data of created study.

splitCharacters.description: Set of characters for splitting on words

responsesAreAllEnglish.description: True if all responses in English, otherwise false

rid.description:
The respondent id, which is unique among Responses in this Question.

This id identifies the respondent in the Study, and that respondent's Response in this Question.

verbatim.description:
The verbatim text of the response, in other words the answer the respondent gave to the question posed.

Required if this is a new respondent, optional if updating an existing response for this respondent.

translation.description: Optional translation of the verbatim text.

notes.description: Optional notes associated with the response.

appliedcodes.description: Array of codes to apply to the response. Note this will ignore any listed codes already applied and will not remove any.

segmentcodes.description: Array of segments of applied codes

The verbatim text of the response, in other words the answer the respondent gave to the question posed.

Required if this is a new respondent, optional if updating an existing response for this respondent.

notes.description: Optional notes associated with the response.

questionResponses.description: Array of questions with their responses to create

responses.description:
The responses to be added or updated.

All strings in these responses will be trimmed of leading and trailing white space.

autoCodeTextMatch.description:
If true, this will attempt to AutoCode responses based on an exact (but case insensitive) text match with previously-coded responses.
This AutoCode will execute first, followed by (if requested) Input ID matching and Regular Expression matching.

autoCodeInputId.description:
If set to True, it will attempt to Auto Code based on a code's Input ID (Closed ends and Other Specifies only).

This AutoCode will execute after the Text Match and before the Regular Expression match, if either is requested.

autoCodeByRegex.description:
If set to True, it will attempt to Auto Code responses via the codes' Regular Expressions.

This AutoCode will execute after the Text Match and Input ID match, if requested.

PostResponsesRequest.description: A request to add Responses to the specified Question, or to update existing Responses.

codebookKey.description: The key of the Codebook for this Question.

responsesAdded.description:
The count of Responses added to the Question.

This is the number of responses in the PutResponsesRequest that had rid values that were not already present in this Question.

existingResponsesUnchanged.description:
The count of existing Responses unchanged.

This is the number of responses in the PutResponsesRequest that had rid values that were already present in this Question.

responsesCodedByTextMatch.description:
The count of responses coded by text match.

This value will always be zero unless autoCodeTextMatch in the request is true.

responsesCodedByInputId.description:
The count of responses coded by input ID.

This value will always be zero unless autoCodeInputId in the request is true.

responsesCodedByRegex.description:
The count of responses coded by regular expression.

This value will always be zero unless autoCodeByRegex in the request is true.

addedResponses.description: The responses added to the question as a result of the POST operation.

PostResponsesResponse.description: Returns information about the disposition of the Responses added.

rid.description:
The respondent id, which is unique among Responses in this Question.

This id identifies the respondent in the Study, and that respondent's Response in this Question.

verbatim.description:
The verbatim text of the response, in other words the answer the respondent gave to the question posed.

Required if this is a new respondent, optional if updating an existing response for this respondent.

translation.description: Optional translation of the verbatim text.

notes.description: Optional notes associated with the response.

appliedcodes.description: Array of codes to apply to the response. Note this will ignore any listed codes already applied and will not remove any.

segmentcodes.description: Array of segments of applied codes

PutResponse.description: A respondent's answer to the question.

rid.description:
The respondent id, which is unique among Responses in this Question.

This id identifies the respondent in the Study, and that respondent's Response in this Question.

verbatim.description:
The verbatim text of the response, in other words the answer the respondent gave to the question posed.

Required if this is a new respondent, optional if updating an existing response for this respondent.

translation.description: Optional translation of the verbatim text.

notes.description: Optional notes associated with the response.

PutResponseBatch.description: Response class for presenting study response data in the request for PUT BATCH operation.

questionResponses.description: Array of questions with their responses to update

allowEmptyStrings.description:
When it is true it allows setting empty string as value for verbatim/translation/transcription/notes fields. 
Otherwise empty string is ignored (i.e. it works like null value). By default it is false.

PutResponsesBatchRequest.description: Request with inforamtion for updating multiple responses via one API call (batch operation)

questionResponses.description: Array of questions with their responses which were added/updated

PutResponsesBatchResponse.description: Response contains information about updated responses in target study questions

errorMessage.description: Error message when updating question responses finished with some error

PutResponsesBatchResult.description: Class contains information about updated responses for target questions

responses.description:
The responses to be added or updated.

All strings in these responses will be trimmed of leading and trailing white space.

codingMethod.description: Method of coding to use for this request.

allowEmptyStrings.description:
When it is true it allows setting empty string as value for verbatim/translation/transcription/notes fields. 
Otherwise empty string is ignored (i.e. it works like null value). By default it is false.

PutResponsesRequest.description: A request to add Responses to the specified Question, or to update existing Responses.

responsesAdded.description:
The count of Responses added to the Question.

This is the number of responses in the PutResponsesRequest that had rid values that were not already present in this Question.

existingResponsesUpdated.description:
The count of existing Responses updated.

This is the number of responses in the PutResponsesRequest that had rid values that were already present in this Question,
and for which any of the text, translation, transcription, or notes values were specified in the request and differed from the existing value in the Response.

existingResponsesUnchanged.description:
The count of existing Responses unchanged.

This is the number of responses in the PutResponsesRequest that had rid values that were already present in this Question,
and for which all of the text, translation, transcription, or notes values were either not specified in the request or identical to the existing value in the Response.

PutResponsesResponse.description: Returns information about the disposition of the Responses added.

codebookKey.description: The key of the Codebook for the Question.

id.description:
The id of the Question.

The id is unique by case-insensitive comparison across all Questions in this Study.

Note that the id may be changed by the user, so while it uniquely identifies the Question at a given time, it is not guaranteed to remain unchanged for the lifetime of the Question.

countResponses.description: The number of Responses in this Question.

countResponsesCoded.description: The number of Responses in this Question that have at least one Code applied.

countTranscriptions.description: The number of Responses in this Question that have transcriptions.

countTranslations.description: The number of Responses in this Question that have translations.

countQualityReviewed.description: The number of Responses in this question that are quality reviewed

label.description:
The label for the Question, which may be empty.

The label is used as a short identifier for the Question in reports when the full id of the Question would be too long.

text.description: The text of the Question posed to the respondent, which may be empty.

help.description: The help information for the Question, which may be empty.

sortOrder.description: Ordinal number of the Question. It uses for sorting questions in list, tables, so on.

ResponseCodeRemoved.description: Contains information about codes that were uncode from responses

isForceToTextOption.description: Indicates if the script output is forced to text format

friendlyName.description: Provides a user-friendly, display-ready name for the script

acceptsQuestionList.description: Specifies if the script can accept a list of questions as a parameter

modifiesData.description: Flags whether running the script will alter or update any data in the database

parameters.description: Script parameters

ordinal.description: Ordinal number. It is used for sorting.

isRequired.description: Indicates whether this parameter is required

ScriptParameter.description: Script parameter definition for Studies API

SegmentCode.description: A descriptor class for code segment

SetQuestionCodebookResponse.description: A response for SetQuestionCodebook web method

key.description: The key of the Study, which will not change for the lifetime of the Study.

quota.description:
The quota for the Study
If no quota is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

name.description:
The name of the Study.

If no study name is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

description.description:
The description of the Study.

If no description is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

status.description:
A one-character string which specifies the status of the Study:
  • a: Archived
  • p: Coding in progress
  • c: Completed
  • d: Deleted
  • h: On hold
  • r: Review in progress
  • s: Shipping
  • u: Under construction

supervisorKey.description:
The key of the Person who will do revision of coding work

If no supervisor is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

clientCompanyKey.description:
The key of the Company for which this coding work is assigned

If no client company is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

endCompanyKey.description:
The key of the Company for which this coding work is being performed

If no end customer company is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

help.description:
The information about the Study

If no help information is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

createDateUtc.description: The UTC date/time the Study was created.

startDateUtc.description:
UTC date/time the Study was started.

If the date is not set yet for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

dueDateUtc.description:
UTC date/time the Study was completed.

If the date is not set yet for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

questionKey.description: Key of question that was translated

translationsCount.description: Number of translations that were done

nonTranslatedResponsesCount.description: Number of responses that were not translated

studyKey.description: Key of a study that was translated

totalTranslationsCount.description: Number of translations that were done

questions.description: Information about the operation grouped by questions

regexPattern.description: Regular expression pattern which may be used for assisting human coders or for automatic coding.

color.description:
Color for the code in CSS hexadecimal six digit format, e.g. #FF0000 for red.

If the code color is the default value of #000000 (black) this field will not be present in the response.

UpdateCode.description:
Class contains information about code and its properties to update. 
Only not-null properties will be updated, i.e. for properties with null value the code saves original value.

codebook.description:
The Codes in the Codebook, in the order they appear in the Codebook.
  
Codebooks are tree structured and may be of any depth, although a depth of more than 6 is rare.
  
Only the leaf nodes may be applied to a response.  These leaf nodes are Codes.

Nets have child codes or nets.  Nets may not be used to code a response.  They serve to provide structure to the Codebook.

Nets typically represent groups of related codes.

failedCodes.description: Codes that were failed while updating by some reason or error.

UpdateCodesInCodebookResponse.description: Response contains information about failed codes. If a code with some key from the request is not presented in failedCodes collection it means that the code was successfully updated.

label.description: The label is used as a short identifier for the Question in reports when the full id of the Question would be too long.

text.description: The text of the Question posed to the respondent, which may be empty.

help.description: The help information for the Question, which may be empty.

sortOrder.description: Ordinal number of the Question. It uses for sorting questions in list, tables, so on.

UpdateQuestionBatch.description: Class contains question properties which should be updated

UpdateQuestionsBatchRequest.description: Request contains collection of questions and their properties to update. Only non-null properties will be updated.

UpdateQuestionsBatchResponse.description: Response contains information about failed questions i.e. questions that could not be updated be some error/reason.

studyKey.description: Key of a study that should be updated.

id.description:
The id of the new Study.

Must be unique among all Studies in the Ascribe account of the current session by case-insensitive comparison.

Must be at least 1 character and not more than 30 characters.

name.description:
Optional name of this Study, intended to be more human-friendly than the id of the Study.

Maximum of 50 characters.

description.description: An optional description of the Study.

status.description:
Optional status of the Study. If specified, one of:
  • a: Archived
  • p: Coding in progress
  • c: Completed
  • d: Deleted
  • h: On hold
  • r: Review in progress
  • s: Shipping
  • u: Under construction

supervisorKey.description:
The key of the Person who will do revision of coding work

If no supervisor is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

clientCompanyKey.description: Optional key of the Company for which this coding work is being performed.

endCompanyKey.description:
The key of the Company for which this coding work is being performed

If no end customer company is set for the Study this field will not be present in the response.

help.description:
Optional text to assist workers coding this Study

This may be used to provide customer specific instructions to the coders for work on this Study.

startDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was started.

dueDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was completed.

UpdateStudyRequest.description: A request to update a new Study.

name.description: A user-friendly display name of the Study.

status.description:
A one-character string which specifies the status of the Study:
  • a: Archived
  • p: Coding in progress
  • c: Completed
  • d: Deleted
  • h: On hold
  • r: Review in progress
  • s: Shipping
  • u: Under construction

supervisorKey.description: The key of the Person who will do revision of coding work

supervisorAssociateKey.description: The key of the supervisor's associate

clientCompanyKey.description: The Company to which this Study is assigned, or null if the Study is not assigned to a Company.

endCompanyKey.description: The key of the Company for which this coding work is being performed

help.description: Information to coders to assist in coding this Study.

createDateUtc.description: The UTC date/time the Study was created.

startDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was started.

dueDateUtc.description: UTC date/time the Study was completed.

countQuestions.description: The number of Questions in the Study.

countResponses.description: The sum of the number of Responses in each Question in the Study.

countResponsesCoded.description: The sum of the number of Responses to which at least one Code has been assigned in each Question in the Study.

countTransactions.description: The number of transactions used by this Study.  Transactions are tokens of work performed on the Study.

UpdateStudyResponse.description: The response with data of created study.

key.description: The key for this user.  The key for a user cannot be changed, and will always identify that user in the account.

id.description: The user's logon id.  This value is unique by case-insensitive comparison across all users in the account.  However, this value may be changed by an administrator of the account.

Bearer.description:
Please enter a valid bearer token.

It should be in the format "bearer eyJhbGciOiJSU..."

It will be added to the "Authentication" HTTP header
